creator: wahjava@gmail.com
text:
On Sunday 17 Aug 2008 8:51:19 pm MANTANI Nobutaka wrote:
> At Sun, 17 Aug 2008 17:31:47 +0530,
>
> Ashish Shukla wrote:
> > Following is my /etc/make.conf
> >
> > (snip)
> >
> > .if ${.CURDIR}==/usr/ports/multimedia/libxine
> > CFLAGS= -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
> > WITH_DEBUG=
> > CPUTYPE=
> > DEBUG_FLAGS=
> > .endif
>
> I found that "-fno-strict-aliasing" option also affects build.
> How about removing it from CFLAGS?

Sorry, but that doesn't fixes the error. BtW, I fixed that error with a 
workaround, which I'm not sure is correct. I'm attaching the diff between 
original port and my workaround-ed port. An issue which I've with my port's 
generated executable is mentioned following:

abbe [~] monte-cristo% ldd `which xine-list-1.1`
/usr/local/bin/xine-list-1.1:
        ../src/xine-engine/.libs/libxine.so (0x0)
        libintl.so.8 => /usr/local/lib/libintl.so.8 (0x800633000)
        libz.so.4 => /lib/libz.so.4 (0x80073f000)
        libm.so.5 => /lib/libm.so.5 (0x800853000)
        libiconv.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so.3 (0x80096d000)
        librt.so.1 => /usr/lib/librt.so.1 (0x800b66000)
        libthr.so.3 => /lib/libthr.so.3 (0x800c6b000)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x800d81000)

BtW, I've built the workaround-ed port with the same default options (no 
changes at all), but I don't think adding those options is causing the above 
issue.

Thanks
Ashish Shukla
